title: Indian Trio Devika, Srikanth, Chouhan Reach US Open Semifinals
indian_angle: Three Indian shuttlers, including Devika Sihag who upset a seeded player, have advanced to the semifinals of the US Open, boosting India's medal prospects.
summary: Devika Sihag caused an upset by defeating third seed Riko Gunji, while Kidambi Srikanth and Rounak Chouhan also secured their spots in the US Open badminton semifinals.

title: Rounak Chouhan Stuns World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a major career milestone by defeating the formidable World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, pulled off a significant upset by defeating World No 6 Chou Tien Chen during his run at the US Open.

title: Macau: Where PV Sindhu Began Her Road to Stardom
indian_angle: This article explores the early career steps of Indian badminton icon PV Sindhu, highlighting the significant role Macau played in her journey to becoming a global star.
summary: A look back at the early career of Indian badminton star PV Sindhu, tracing her initial steps on the path to stardom in the unlikely setting of Macau.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games, Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The Indian badminton squad, including the returning Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, is reassembling to prepare for the upcoming Asian Games, signaling India's strong medal ambitions.
summary: The Indian badminton squad that competed in the Thomas Cup is reassembling for the Asian Games, with the notable return of the women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.

title: Tanvi Sharma's Game: A Comparison to Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu
indian_angle: This article delves into the playing style of young Indian talent Tanvi Sharma, drawing comparisons to the legendary careers of Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.
summary: An analysis of rising Indian badminton player Tanvi Sharma's game, exploring whether her development aligns more closely with the aggressive style of Saina Nehwal or the defensive prowess of PV Sindhu.

title: Satwik-Chirag Crowned Singapore Open Champions After Thrilling Final
indian_angle: India's top men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, secured a prestigious Super 750 title at the Singapore Open with a dominant performance.
summary: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty clinched the Singapore Open title, showcasing furious rallies, powerful smashes, and their signature celebratory dances in a memorable final performance.
